textPlain: It does seem like judicial activism. I’m not sure what in the law permits the judge to arbitrarily decide that. But the tone of what she said (“it’s not a hint” etc) is aggressive and unprofessional, which makes me think it is judicial activism like the DHS alleges. Also the same judge said they can’t use riot control techniques like tear gas, even though the agents are clearly facing violence and riots in some situations, which again seems arbitrary.
Requiring police to wear and use body cameras is judicial activism to achieve...?
